The classification of Stenostomum tomentosum places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida.

intro: Stenostomum tomentosum, also known as "Santa Cruz de Flor de Mayo" or "Tomentose Stenostomum", is a shrub native to Mexico, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Stenostomum tomentosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
